message:
fix(aider): stream history files, preserve existing content on write, fix workspace extraction

Three related fixes to the Aider provider:

1. Stream-parse history files in owns_session() and list_sessions()
   instead of loading entire files into memory via read_to_string +
   split_sessions(). Aider history files can grow very large since
   multiple sessions are appended to a single file. The new approach
   uses BufReader line-by-line scanning with parse_session_header(),
   matching session IDs without materializing the full file contents.

2. Fix write_session() to preserve existing history file contents
   when writing. Previously, atomic_write would overwrite the target
   file, destroying all prior sessions in that history file. Now the
   method reads existing content first and prepends it, then forces
   the write (force=true) since a pre-existing file is the expected
   state for Aider's append-based format, not a conflict.

3. Fix extract_workspace_from_tool_line() off-by-one: the guard
   condition was `path.len() > prefix.len() + 2`, which would reject
   short but valid workspace paths (e.g., "/home/a"). Changed to
   `path.len() > prefix.len()` to accept any path with at least one
   character after the prefix. Also removes a trailing blank line
   before the #[cfg(test)] block.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
